title: CIA: MDR: The Iranian Mine Warfare Threat - 1984

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CIA: MDR: The Iranian Mine Warfare Threat - 1984
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Section 3.5 of Executive Order 13526 and the implementing regulations of the Central Intelligence Agency (32 C.F.R. Part 1908), I hereby submit this request for Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) of the following document:

Title: "The Iranian Mine Warfare Threat"
Document Identifier: CIA-RDP85T00314R000300100002-7
Date of Document: November 1984
Online location: https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP85T00314R000300100002-7.pdf

This document ("The Iranian Mine Warfare Threat" dated November 1984) has been partially released through the CIA's CREST (CIA Records Search Tool) database, indicating that the Agency has already acknowledged its existence and released portions of it. This MDR requests a systematic, line-by-line re-review of every remaining redaction, with particular scrutiny of those citing the 25X1 exemption.

This MDR respectfully requests the following:
1. A line-by-line review of every 25X1 redaction in document CIA-RDP85T00314R000300100002-7 applying the current standard: whether release of the specific redacted information — not the source identity itself — would cause identifiable, current damage to national security.
2. That reviewers apply the principle established in EO 13526 Section 3.5(d): where doubt exists about whether harm would result from declassification, the doubt shall be resolved in favor of declassification.
3. Full declassification of the Iraqi Mine Warfare sidebar, as the governmental and military entities described no longer exist.
4. Full declassification of all geographic, technical, and equipment specifications that are reproducible from open sources, including the mine specifications in Figure 1 and the geographic analysis in Figure 2.
5. Declassification of all forward-looking projections from 1984 that have since been confirmed or refuted by observable historical events.
6. Where source identities genuinely must remain protected, release of the surrounding analytical text with the source line alone redacted, rather than redaction of entire analytical conclusions.

Thank you for the Central Intelligence Agency's commitment to transparency. I look forward to working towards having this review resolved.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: RE: CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST – SPECIFIC FINANCIAL INSTRUMENT RECEIPT (Santa Monica College)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the Respective Coordinators,

Under the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code § 7920.000 et seq.), I hereby request the disclosure of the following records. Given the history of technical denials, I demand a full search for the following:

1. Specific Financial Disposition (SMC District)

Receipt Records: All logs/entries confirming receipt of USPS Tracking #9589071052701840988203 (Delivered Feb 24, 2026) addressed to Robert M. Myers at 1900 Pico Blvd.

Usage/Allocation: If the $0.60 check was deposited, provide the fund allocation and expenditure records.

Rejection Justification: If not deposited, provide the internal memo or policy citing the reason for rejection/non-deposit.

2. Historical Pattern of Administrative Bias (Young Jin Jung)

Denial Logs: A comprehensive list of all prior payments or CPRA requests from "Young Jin Jung" that were rejected.

Nomenclature Precedents: Records showing denials based on minor clerical issues (e.g., "SMCC" vs. full District name) to determine if the District is engaging in pretextual obstruction.

3. Characterization & Framing Logs (UC Berkeley/UCPD)

"Idolization" Rhetoric: Any and all internal communications (emails, Slack, memos) where the term "idolize" or similar framing was used to characterize my inquiries regarding the 2013 SMC shooting.

Retaliatory Reporting: All records of communication with external government/law enforcement agencies regarding my person, including which agency was dispatched and the specific justification/dispatch logs provided.

Victim Status Acknowledgment: Records discussing my status as a victim/survivor of the 2013 shooting and any decision to disregard this status in administrative dealings.

Legal Notice: * Redaction Mandate: Per Gov. Code § 7922.525, you must redact sensitive info and release the remainder; a blanket denial is a violation.

Duty to Assist: Per Gov. Code § 7922.600, you are obligated to assist in narrowing this search if deemed "overbroad."

Deadline: A determination is required within 10 days.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

title: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

TO: Freedom of Information Officer

Park District of La Grange

536 East Avenue

La Grange, Illinois 60525

DATE: March 11, 2026

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request for public records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting the following records regarding tree removal and forestry management at Sedgwick Park:

Work Orders and Contracts: All documents, work orders, or contracts with third-party landscaping or arboricultural services regarding the removal of trees at Sedgwick Park from January 1, 2025, to the present.

Internal Assessments: Any arborists' reports, tree health evaluations, or staff memos recommending or justifying the removal of specific trees within Sedgwick Park.

Correspondence: All correspondence (including emails, letters, and internal memos) between Park District staff, Board Commissioners, and/or contractors concerning tree removal, stump grinding, or planned reforestation/replanting at Sedgwick Park.

Financial Records: All invoices or receipts related to the costs incurred for these tree removal services.

Request for Fee Waiver (Public Interest)
Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6(c), I am requesting a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is in the public interest because:

Public Asset Management: Sedgwick Park is a significant public resource. Information regarding the removal of its natural canopy is of high interest to the community.

Non-Commercial: The records are being sought to inform the public and are not for personal or commercial gain.

Environmental Impact: Understanding the management of the "Urban Forest" within local parks relates directly to the welfare of the residents and the maintenance of public land.

Instructions for Response
Format: Please provide these records in an electronic format to the email address provided below.

Statutory Deadlines: As required by 5 ILCS 140/3(d), if the Park District fails to respond within the five-business-day window (or a valid extension period), the District may not impose fees and may not treat this request as "unduly burdensome."

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption for each redaction and provide the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

Thank you,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

Disregard information below this line:

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

Village of La Grange

53 S. La Grange Road

La Grange, IL 60525

DATE: March 11, 2026

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request for public records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting the following records regarding Plan Commission Cases 284, 285, 286, 287, 288, and 289:

Reimbursable Charge Records: All documents identifying or listing reimbursable charges, actual costs, or fees incurred by the Village in processing these specific applications.

Invoices: All complete invoices (including those from professional and technical consultants, legal counsel, court reporters, or secretarial services) associated with these cases.

Correspondence: All correspondence regarding the assessment, billing, or collection of these charges between Village staff, the applicants, the property owners, or third-party consultants.

Request for Fee Waiver (Public Interest)
Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6(c), I am requesting a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is in the public interest because:

Dissemination to the Public: The principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the fiscal management and legal transparency of the Village’s Plan Commission proceedings to the general public.

Non-Commercial: This request is not for personal or commercial benefit.

Public Welfare: Understanding how public resources and reimbursable expenses are managed directly impacts the welfare and legal rights of the residents of La Grange.

Instructions for Response
Format: Please provide these records in an electronic format (PDF/Excel) to the email address provided below.

Statutory Deadlines: As a reminder, under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), if the Village fails to respond within the statutory timeframe, it forfeits the right to charge fees and may not treat this request as "unduly burdensome" under Section 3(g).

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption for each redaction and provide the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

Thank you,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: Del Galdo

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Del Galdo
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Since January 1, 2025 through current:

1) Copies of all invoices from the Del Galdo law firm;

2) Copies of records of payments by CPS/Chicago Board of Education to Del Galdo law firm. I want the actual bills as well as proof of payment by the district.

3) Any and all letters of engagement, if changed from previous engagement letters, between CPS/Chicago Board of Education and Del Galdo law firm, including any modifications or changes.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H(b): Current ALPR-Equipped Vehicle Inventory
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The current list of Seattle Police Department vehicles equipped with Automated License Plate Reader systems, including the license plate number, equipment number, vehicle year, make, model, and assigned organizational unit for each vehicle.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

title: GORA Request: Police Reports, Investigations, and Associated Records

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Georgia Open Records Request – Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

To: Records Division, DeKalb County Police Department

Date: March 8, 2026

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I request all public records in the possession of the DeKalb County Police Department relating to myself, Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., including but not limited to:

1. 	Incident Reports / Case Files
All incident, case, or supplemental reports referencing me.
Internal notes, investigative assignments, and progress updates, including those created or submitted by private investigators or assigned detectives.
Any associated contractor, vendor, or third-party service provider involvement in these cases.

2. 	911 / Dispatch Records
Audio recordings, transcripts, and CAD logs referencing me, my addresses, PO Box, or phone numbers.

3. 	Body-Worn Camera / Dash-cam Footage
All recordings in which I appear or that relate to incidents concerning me.

4. 	Communications and Internal Records
Emails, memoranda, internal communications, and any correspondence mentioning me.
Evidence logs, attachments, photographs, and related documentation.
References to any contractors, vendors, or third-party service providers used in the investigation, handling, or processing of these records.

5. 	Third-Party Reports
Complaints, statements, or calls from any persons reporting on me, including witnesses, neighbors, or other third parties.

6. 	Investigator Assignments
Records showing which personnel were assigned to investigate matters involving me.
Records or memos referencing any external contractors, vendors, or service providers assigned to assist in these investigations.
Records referencing the following phone numbers:
404-731-1163
470-591-7000
404-389-5642

8. 	Addresses / Time Periods
Records relating to the following addresses and time periods:
1261 Lavista Road NE, E-17, Atlanta, GA 30324 – 2000 to 2004
1514 Sheridan Road NE, #4408, Atlanta, GA 30324 – 2015 to 2025
2204 Lavista Road NE, #2298-13, Atlanta, GA 30329 – 2015 to 2025
2897 North Druid Hills Road NE, #385, Atlanta, GA 30329 - 2015 to 2025

9. Additional Requests
Any references to contractors, vendors, or third-party service providers involved in investigations, data collection, evidence handling, or administrative processing related to the records described above.
Any documentation of agreements, contracts, memoranda, or correspondence with such entities in connection with cases involving me.

I am requesting all records that pertain to me, Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., including records in which I may be identified by any known variants of my name, including Charles S. DeLozier, Charles DeLozier Jr., or my commonly used name, Chuck DeLozier. This request also includes any records in which I may be identified by other personal identifiers associated with me, such as phone numbers, addresses, or other identifying information. I do not seek private or personal information about other individuals except as it appears in official records relating to me.

This request is intended to obtain public records under the Georgia Open Records Act and does not request any material outside the department’s official documentation.

Please provide records in electronic format if possible. If fees are anticipated, please notify me before processing.

Thank you for your attention and assistance in providing these records.

Sincerely, 
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.
